task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated clinical diagnosis. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist of the most relevant and methodologically rigorous papers.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML method applied directly to automated clinical diagnosis (disease detection, imaging diagnosis, or differential diagnosis from patient data). Prognosis, treatment selection, drug discovery, and methodology papers without a diagnostic application should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague method; 3=clear method with metrics; 4=full method with baselines and ablations.

papers.title: CNN for Automated Pneumonia Detection in Chest Radiographs
papers.abstract: We train a ResNet-50 convolutional neural network on 112,000 chest X-rays labelled for pneumonia. The model achieves AUC 0.95 on a prospective test set and matches the sensitivity of three radiologists in a reader study. Ablation analysis confirms the value of data augmentation and class-balanced sampling.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, radiology, pneumonia detection
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50, AUC 0.95, radiologist reader study, ablation study
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet-50 pneumonia detector matching radiologist sensitivity with a full ablation study.

papers.title: Gradient Boosting for 30-Day ICU Mortality Prediction
papers.abstract: Machine learning for clinical diagnosis has expanded dramatically. We apply gradient boosting to predict 30-day mortality risk for ICU patients using admission vitals and lab results, achieving AUC 0.83. The system is intended to assist with palliative care consultations, not to provide a diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: machine learning, ICU, mortality prediction, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, ICU vitals, AUC 0.83, prognosis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting model predicting 30-day ICU mortality for palliative care prioritisation.

papers.title: BERT Fine-Tuning for ICD Diagnosis Code Assignment from Clinical Notes
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a BERT model on 240,000 discharge summaries to assign ICD-10 diagnosis codes from free-text clinical notes. The system achieves micro-F1 of 0.82 on the MIMIC-III benchmark, outperforming CNN and LSTM baselines. Model weights and a replication package are publicly released.
papers.topic_hint: NLP, clinical notes, ICD coding, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: BERT fine-tuning, MIMIC-III, micro-F1 0.82, CNN and LSTM baselines, public code
papers.claimed_contribution: BERT ICD-10 coder with micro-F1 0.82 on MIMIC-III, outperforming all prior baselines.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Antibiotic Selection in ICU Sepsis
papers.abstract: We apply deep reinforcement learning to recommend antibiotic regimens for sepsis patients. Using a retrospective cohort of 18,000 ICU episodes, the RL policy reduces 28-day mortality by 11% relative to standard of care in off-policy evaluation. This is a treatment selection task, not diagnostic.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, sepsis, treatment selection
papers.methodology_hint: deep RL, off-policy evaluation, 18000 episodes, mortality reduction
papers.claimed_contribution: RL antibiotic policy reducing 28-day sepsis mortality by 11% in retrospective cohort.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Skin Lesion Classification Across Seven Dermoscopy Classes
papers.abstract: We train an EfficientNet-B4 on 25,000 dermoscopy images across 7 lesion classes including melanoma. The model achieves balanced accuracy of 0.87, matching board-certified dermatologists on a held-out test set, with full ablation, confidence calibration, and inter-rater reliability analysis.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, dermatology, lesion classification, melanoma diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B4, balanced accuracy 0.87, dermatologist comparison, ablation, calibration
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet skin lesion classifier matching dermatologist accuracy with full ablation and calibration.

papers.title: Graph Neural Networks for Drug-Target Interaction Prediction
papers.abstract: Predicting drug-target binding affinity is central to drug discovery. We develop a graph neural network trained on ChEMBL to predict interaction scores for novel pairs. The model achieves Pearson r=0.87 on a held-out benchmark and outperforms random forest and SVM baselines.
papers.topic_hint: drug discovery, GNN, binding affinity prediction
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, ChEMBL, Pearson correlation, drug-target affinity, RF and SVM baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for drug-target binding prediction with Pearson r=0.87 on held-out benchmark.

papers.title: Transformer-Based Arrhythmia Diagnosis from 12-Lead ECG Signals
papers.abstract: We apply a transformer architecture to 12-lead ECG signals for automated arrhythmia diagnosis across 20 classes. Trained on 500,000 ECGs from three hospital systems, the model achieves macro-F1 of 0.88. We compare against CNN and LSTM baselines and perform per-class ablation of attention heads.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, ECG, arrhythmia, cardiac diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, ECG, macro-F1 0.88, multi-site training, CNN/LSTM comparison, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer ECG classifier with macro-F1 0.88 across 20 arrhythmia classes with full ablation.

papers.title: Bayesian Optimisation for Hyperparameter Tuning in Medical Imaging CNNs
papers.abstract: We present a Bayesian optimisation framework for automated hyperparameter selection in medical imaging neural networks. Applied to three public imaging datasets, the framework reduces tuning wall-clock time by 60% while matching test AUC versus grid search. No new diagnostic model is proposed.
papers.topic_hint: AutoML, hyperparameter optimisation, medical imaging
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian optimisation, AUC, grid search comparison, wall-clock, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian optimisation reduces medical imaging model tuning time by 60% vs grid search.

papers.title: Systematic Review of ML in Clinical Diagnosis and Prognosis
papers.abstract: We review 180 published papers on machine learning in clinical medicine, covering diagnosis and prognosis. Diagnostic ML papers report higher AUCs on average but use less rigorous experimental designs. We identify methodological gaps and recommend reporting standards.
papers.topic_hint: systematic review, clinical ML, reporting standards
papers.methodology_hint: systematic review, meta-analysis, AUC comparison, methodology audit
papers.claimed_contribution: Systematic review of 180 clinical ML papers with reporting standard recommendations.

papers.title: GAN Augmentation for Rare Disease Diagnostic Imaging
papers.abstract: Training data scarcity limits diagnostic ML for rare diseases. We use generative adversarial networks to synthesise pathology images for three rare genetic conditions. A diagnostic CNN trained with GAN augmentation improves macro-F1 from 0.51 to 0.71 on real patient test data.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, data augmentation, rare disease, diagnostic imaging
papers.methodology_hint: GAN synthesis, CNN, macro-F1 improvement from 0.51 to 0.71
papers.claimed_contribution: GAN augmentation improves rare-disease diagnostic CNN macro-F1 from 0.51 to 0.71.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Multi-Site Hospital Readmission Prediction
papers.abstract: We propose a federated learning framework for training readmission prediction models across 8 hospital sites without sharing patient data. The federated model matches the centralised baseline AUC of 0.79 while preserving privacy. Readmission prediction is a prognosis task.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, privacy, readmission prediction, multi-site
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, differential privacy, AUC 0.79, 8 sites, prognosis task
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated readmission predictor matching centralised AUC with differential privacy.

papers.title: Graph Neural Networks for Rare Metabolic Disease Diagnosis from EHR
papers.abstract: We represent each patient's EHR as a heterogeneous graph over diagnoses, labs, and medications, and train a GNN to flag rare lysosomal storage disorders. Trained on 2.1 million records, the model achieves precision 0.79 and recall 0.81. GNN results are compared to rule-based and logistic regression baselines.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, EHR, rare disease, metabolic disorder, clinical diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: heterogeneous GNN, EHR graph, precision 0.79, recall 0.81, rule-based and LR comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN rare-disease detector from EHR with precision 0.79 and recall 0.81, baseline comparison.

papers.title: AlphaFold Extension for Intrinsically Disordered Protein Regions
papers.abstract: We extend AlphaFold2 to predict structures of intrinsically disordered protein regions using conditional generation. Our approach achieves TM-score improvements of 0.12 over the baseline on a benchmark of 500 disordered regions. No clinical diagnostic application is presented.
papers.topic_hint: protein structure, AlphaFold, structural biology
papers.methodology_hint: AlphaFold extension, conditional generation, TM-score, structural biology only
papers.claimed_contribution: AlphaFold extension for disordered protein regions improving TM-score by 0.12.

papers.title: Continual Learning for Adapting Clinical NLP to New Hospital Sites
papers.abstract: Clinical NLP models degrade at new institutions due to vocabulary shift. We propose a continual learning approach that adapts a diagnosis extraction model to new sites using 500 labelled notes, without catastrophic forgetting. F1 on the source domain is preserved within 2% after adaptation.
papers.topic_hint: continual learning, clinical NLP, domain adaptation
papers.methodology_hint: continual learning, catastrophic forgetting prevention, F1 preservation, few-shot
papers.claimed_contribution: Continual learning adapter for clinical NLP preserving source-domain F1 within 2%.

papers.title: Random Forest for Diabetic Retinopathy Grading from Fundus Features
papers.abstract: We extract hand-crafted features from fundus photographs and train a random forest to grade diabetic retinopathy at five severity levels. On 8,000 test images, the model achieves quadratic weighted kappa of 0.83. We compare against a deep learning baseline and analyse feature importance.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, diabetic retinopathy, fundus grading, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, hand-crafted fundus features, quadratic kappa 0.83, DL comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest retinopathy grader with kappa 0.83 and deep learning comparison.

papers.title: Optimal Transport for Staining Domain Adaptation in Histopathology
papers.abstract: Histopathology models degrade across staining protocols. We apply optimal transport to align feature distributions across staining domains for tumour detection. AUC improves from 0.72 to 0.88 after adaptation on three public histopathology benchmarks.
papers.topic_hint: optimal transport, domain adaptation, histopathology, tumour detection
papers.methodology_hint: optimal transport, AUC 0.72 to 0.88, three benchmarks, staining adaptation
papers.claimed_contribution: Optimal transport domain adaptation improves histopathology tumour AUC from 0.72 to 0.88.

papers.title: Regulatory and Ethical Frameworks for Clinical ML Deployment
papers.abstract: This position paper discusses regulatory pathways and ethical obligations for deploying ML diagnostic systems. We review FDA guidance, CE marking requirements, and post-market surveillance standards. No new ML model or empirical evaluation is presented.
papers.topic_hint: regulatory, ethics, clinical ML, FDA
papers.methodology_hint: regulatory review, ethics analysis, no empirical ML evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: Review of regulatory and ethical frameworks for clinical ML deployment.

papers.title: Neural Survival Models for Cancer Recurrence Prognosis
papers.abstract: We apply a neural network survival model to predict cancer recurrence after surgical resection in colorectal cancer. Using pathology reports and molecular markers, the model achieves concordance index 0.78, outperforming Cox regression. Recurrence prediction is a prognostic task.
papers.topic_hint: survival analysis, neural networks, cancer prognosis, recurrence
papers.methodology_hint: neural survival model, concordance index 0.78, Cox regression comparison, prognosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Neural survival model for colorectal cancer recurrence with concordance index 0.78.

papers.title: Survey of Attention Mechanisms in Medical Image Segmentation
papers.abstract: We survey 95 papers on attention mechanisms in medical image segmentation across CT, MRI, and histopathology. We provide a taxonomy, benchmark comparison table, and identify open challenges. No new model is proposed and no diagnostic task is evaluated.
papers.topic_hint: survey, attention, medical image segmentation
papers.methodology_hint: survey, taxonomy, benchmark table, no new model, no diagnostic evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of attention mechanisms in medical image segmentation covering 95 papers.

papers.title: Contrastive Self-Supervised Pre-training for ECG Representation Learning
papers.abstract: We present a contrastive self-supervised pre-training strategy for ECG encoders using 2 million unlabelled ECGs. The pre-trained encoder, fine-tuned on small labelled sets, outperforms supervised baselines on arrhythmia classification and ST-elevation detection by 4-7% AUC using only 1% of labels.
papers.topic_hint: self-supervised learning, ECG, contrastive pre-training, arrhythmia
papers.methodology_hint: contrastive pre-training, label-efficient fine-tuning, AUC improvement, ECG diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive ECG pre-training for label-efficient arrhythmia and ST-elevation detection.
